title: AI Evals - Part 2: Mastering Deterministic Evals
iq: How do you test whether an AI feature is working correctly before shipping to production? / What is the difference between deterministic and probabilistic evals, and when do you use each?
kt: Deterministic evals use code (not LLMs) to check facts, numbers, and hard constraints — they are free, instant, and 100% accurate. The rule: use Python for math, use LLMs for vibes. Force AI output to JSON, then write unit tests for JSON validity, constraint satisfaction, and hallucination detection via database lookup.
cc:
THE CORE DISTINCTION

Two types of evals, two different jobs:

  • Probabilistic (LLM-as-Judge) — send your AI's answer to another LLM and ask "is this good?" Understands nuance and tone. Slow, expensive, and makes mistakes. Use only for subjective quality like "was this easy to understand?"
  • Deterministic (Code) — use Python to check facts, numbers, and hard constraints. Free, instant, 100% accurate. Cannot judge tone. Use for everything measurable.
PM decision: Use Python for Math, use LLMs for Vibes. LLMs are confident liars — never ask an LLM to check whether a price is under ₹500 when a single if statement does it perfectly.
WHY THIS MATTERS — THE FOODIE APP FAILURE

User asks for a chicken burger under ₹500. LLM recommends the Spicy Chicken Burger at ₹350. User clicks order. Actual price: ₹550. User deletes the app.

The LLM hallucinated the price to be helpful. It wanted to satisfy the user's constraint, so it lied. Deterministic evals catch this before the user ever sees it.

THE THREE UNIT TESTS EVERY AI PM SHOULD DEMAND
  • JSON Validity Rate — did the AI output parseable JSON at all? Target: 99.9% pass rate. If output isn't valid JSON, the app crashes.
  • Constraint Satisfaction Rate — did the AI follow the rules? User said "under ₹500" → check if response['price'] <= 500. Binary. No maybe. Either it followed the rule or it didn't.
  • Hallucination Rate (Inventory Check) — is the recommended item real? Take dish_name from AI response → SELECT * FROM menu WHERE name = 'X'. If 0 results, the AI invented a fake product.
STEP 1 — FORCE JSON OUTPUT

You cannot automate testing on a blob of text. The first thing to demand from engineering: AI must output structured JSON, not prose.

Bad: "The Maharaja Mac is a great option. It costs 240 rupees."

Good: {"dish_name": "Maharaja Mac", "price": 240, "is_vegetarian": false}

Once you have JSON, you don't need AI to check the answer — code does it instantly and for free.

THE LIMIT OF DETERMINISTIC EVALS

Code checks math. It cannot check quality. An AI can recommend a burger that is ₹200 (passes price test), exists in the database (passes inventory test), but describes itself as "made of wet cardboard and sadness." Code says PASS. User experience is terrible.

That's where LLM-as-a-Judge comes in — for the subjective layer that code cannot reach. Part 3 covers this.

PM interview signal: candidates who can articulate which eval type fits which problem show genuine AI product maturity. Most candidates conflate the two.
THE PM QUESTION TO ASK YOUR TEAM TOMORROW

"Are we parsing the AI response as JSON, and do we have automated checks for numerical limits?"

If the answer is No — you are not ready for production. This is the minimum bar before any AI feature ships.
